2026-03-13T11:52:32.2287585Z 
2026-03-13T11:52:32.2558921Z ● safeoutputs-add_labels
2026-03-13T11:52:32.2559457Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-03-13T11:52:32.2559701Z 
2026-03-13T11:53:01.7714473Z ● safeoutputs-add_comment
2026-03-13T11:53:01.7716814Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-03-13T11:53:01.7717158Z 
2026-03-13T11:53:05.4600734Z Labels applied (`Tables`, `Client`, `customer-reported`, `needs-team-attention`, `bug`, `needs-team-triage`) and triage comment posted on issue #57079.
2026-03-13T11:53:05.4601405Z 
2026-03-13T11:53:05.4601516Z **Summary of findings:**
2026-03-13T11:53:06.4476487Z - The bug is in the protocol method path in `TableRestClient.cs` (custom, non-generated) where `using HttpMessage message` disposes the `HttpMessage` (and its `ContentStream`) after `ProcessMessageAsync` returns — before `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` in `GetEntityInternalAsync` reads the stream.
2026-03-13T11:53:07.7581620Z - The generated `TableRestClient.cs` handles this correctly by deserializing inside the `using` scope; only the custom protocol path is affected.
2026-03-13T11:53:07.7593212Z - Possible fix: use `BinaryData`/buffered content in `SerializationHelpers`, or deserialize before the stream lifetime ends in `GetEntityInternalAsync`.
2026-03-13T11:53:08.4772931Z - CODEOWNERS for `/sdk/tables/` points to `@christothes` and `@jorgerangel-msft` who should be engaged on this.

2026-03-13T11:53:23.0762724Z Agent: **Summary of findings:**
2026-03-13T11:53:23.0764709Z Agent: - The bug is in the protocol method path in `TableRestClient.cs` (custom, non-generated) where `using HttpMessage message` disposes the `HttpMessage` (and its `ContentStream`) after `ProcessMessageAsync` returns — before `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` in `GetEntityInternalAsync` reads the stream.

Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. 2026-03-13T11:53:23.2567184Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T11:51:04.687298281Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57079,\"title\":\"[BUG] System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream when using TableClient\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Data.Tables 12.11.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen calling operations on `TableClient` (e.g. `GetEntityAsync`, `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`), a `System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` is thrown when the entity exists (i.e. the server returns 200 OK).\\n\\nThe root cause is inside `TableRestClient.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync`:\\n`using HttpMessage message = CreateQueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyRequest(...);\\nreturn await _pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);`\\n\\n After the HTTP response is received, the method passes the response stream to `SerializationHelpers` for OData JSON deserialization. However, the `HttpMessage` (and its underlying response `Stream`) is disposed by the Azure SDK pipeline\\u0026#39;s `using` block before the stream has been fully consumed by the deserializer. By the time `SerializationHelpers` attempts to read the remaining bytes, the stream is already closed, causing the `ObjectDisposedException`.\\n\\nThis is a scope-lifetime bug: the pipeline disposes the HttpMessage at the boundary of the transport layer, but the deserialization of the response body happens after that boundary returns — meaning the stream is read outside the lifetime of the object that owns it.\\n\\nThe stack trace looks like this:\\n\\n```\\nSystem.InvalidOperationException: (...) Details: Cannot access a closed Stream., Type = Error, Exception = System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream.\\n   at System.IO.MemoryStream.Read(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 count)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.ReadToEnd(Stream stream)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(Stream utf8Json, JsonDocumentOptions options)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync[T](Boolean async, String partitionKey, String rowKey, Boolean noThrow, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync[T](String partitionKey, String rowKey, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n...\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`TableClient` operations should successfully return the deserialized entity wrapped in a `Response`. No exception should be thrown.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nAn `ObjectDisposedException` is thrown with the message \\u0026#34;Cannot access a closed Stream\\u0026#34;, originating from within `SerializationHelpers` in the `Azure.Data.Tables` library.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Set up an Azure Storage account (or Azurite local emulator) with a table containing at least one entity.\\n2. Create a `TableClient` instance pointing to that table.\\n3. Call `GetEntityAsync(partitionKey, rowKey)`.\\n4. Observe that `ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` is thrown on every invocation.\\n\\n**Minimal repro:**\\n\\n```\\nvar tableClient = new TableClient(connectionString, tableName);\\nawait tableClient.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n\\nawait tableClient.AddEntityAsync(new TableEntity(\\u0026#34;pk\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;rk-1\\u0026#34;));\\n\\n// Throws System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream\\nvar response = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync(\\u0026#34;pk\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;rk-1\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nWindows 10 x64, .NET 10, JetBrains Rider 2025.3.3\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57079\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"RitaNobregaGomes\",\"id\":155727391,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/RitaNobregaGomes\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/155727391?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-13T11:49:03Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-13T11:49:03Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-13T11:53:23.2582840Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T11:51:14.9332833Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"perPage":5,"query":"repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net TableClient ObjectDisposedException closed stream"},"name":"search_issues"}}}

2026-03-13T11:53:23.2610241Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T11:51:15.251837233Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4070619340,\"number\":57079,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream when using TableClient\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Data.Tables 12.11.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen calling operations on `TableClient` (e.g. `GetEntityAsync`, `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`), a `System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` is thrown when the entity exists (i.e. the server returns 200 OK).\\n\\nThe root cause is inside `TableRestClient.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync`:\\n`using HttpMessage message = CreateQueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyRequest(...);\\nreturn await _pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);`\\n\\n After the HTTP response is received, the method passes the response stream to `SerializationHelpers` for OData JSON deserialization. However, the `HttpMessage` (and its underlying response `Stream`) is disposed by the Azure SDK pipeline's `using` block before the stream has been fully consumed by the deserializer. By the time `SerializationHelpers` attempts to read the remaining bytes, the stream is already closed, causing the `ObjectDisposedException`.\\n\\nThis is a scope-lifetime bug: the pipeline disposes the HttpMessage at the boundary of the transport layer, but the deserialization of the response body happens after that boundary returns — meaning the stream is read outside the lifetime of the object that owns it.\\n\\nThe stack trace looks like this:\\n\\n```\\nSystem.InvalidOperationException: (...) Details: Cannot access a closed Stream., Type = Error, Exception = System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream.\\n   at System.IO.MemoryStream.Read(Byte[] buffer, Int32 offset, Int32 count)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.ReadToEnd(Stream stream)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(Stream utf8Json, JsonDocumentOptions options)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync[T](Boolean async, String partitionKey, String rowKey, Boolean noThrow, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync[T](String partitionKey, String rowKey, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n...\\n```\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`TableClient` operations should successfully return the deserialized entity wrapped in a `Response\\u003cT\\u003e`. No exception should be thrown.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nAn `ObjectDisposedException` is thrown with the message \\\"Cannot access a closed Stream\\\", originating from within `SerializationHelpers` in the `Azure.Data.Tables` library.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n1. Set up an Azure Storage account (or Azurite local emulator) with a table containing at least one entity.\\n2. Create a `TableClient` instance pointing to that table.\\n3. Call `GetEntityAsync\\u003cTableEntity\\u003e(partitionKey, rowKey)`.\\n4. Observe that `ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` is thrown on every invocation.\\n\\n**Minimal repro:**\\n\\n```\\nvar tableClient = new TableClient(connectionString, tableName);\\nawait tableClient.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\\n\\nawait tableClient.AddEntityAsync(new TableEntity(\\\"pk\\\", \\\"rk-1\\\"));\\n\\n// Throws System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream\\nvar response = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync\\u003cTableEntity\\u003e(\\\"pk\\\", \\\"rk-1\\\");\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nWindows 10 x64, .NET 10, JetBrains Rider 2026-03-13T11:53:23.2683119Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T11:53:01.754477767Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in `Azure.Data.Tables` where `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync` throw `System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream` when the entity exists (HTTP 200 OK). The reporter is on .NET 10 with `Azure.Data.Tables 12.11.0`.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe bug lives in the interaction between the **protocol method** path in `TableRestClient.cs` (the custom, non-generated version) and `GetEntityInternalAsync` in `TableClient.cs`.\n\n**Protocol method path** (`sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableRestClient.cs`, line ~187-188):\n```csharp\nusing HttpMessage message = CreateQueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyRequest(...);\nreturn await _pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context).ConfigureAwait(false);\n```\n\nWhen `ProcessMessageAsync` returns, the `using` block disposes the `HttpMessage`. If `HttpMessage.Dispose()` disposes the underlying `Response` (and hence its `ContentStream`), the stream is closed at method return.\n\n**Consumer side** (`sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs`, line ~797):\n```csharp\nResponse response = await _tableOperations.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync(...);\n// ... \nvar dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response); // stream already disposed!\n```\n\nAnd in `SerializationHelpers.cs` (line ~14):\n```csharp\nusing var document = JsonDocument.Parse(response.ContentStream); // ← ObjectDisposedException\n```\n\nThe stack trace confirms the `MemoryStream` path — meaning Azure.Core buffers the response into a `MemoryStream`, but that `MemoryStream` is being disposed before `SerializationHelpers` reads it.\n\n**Note:** The *generated* `TableRestClient.cs` (in `Generated/`) correctly deserializes the stream *inside* the `using HttpMessage` scope. Only the protocol method path (non-generated) is affected.\n\n**Possible .NET 10 angle:** This may be a regression — either in a recent Azure.Core update (where `HttpMessage.Dispose()` behavior changed), or a .NET 10–specific change in how `MemoryStream` disposal is handled. The fix may also have existed on earlier .NET versions where the `MemoryStream` was not disposed.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🧪 Reproduction Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n```csharp\nvar tableClient = new TableClient(connectionString, tableName);\nawait tableClient.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();\nawait tableClient.AddEntityAsync(new TableEntity(\"pk\", \"rk-1\"));\n\n// Throws System.ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a closed Stream\nvar response = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync\u003cTableEntity\u003e(\"pk\", \"rk-1\");\n```\n\nCan also reproduce with Azurite emulator locally. Affected methods:\n- `GetEntityAsync\u003cT\u003e`\n- `GetEntityIfExistsAsync\u003cT\u003e`\n- Any path calling `GetEntityInternalAsync\u003cT\u003e`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Debugging Strategy\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Verify `HttpMessage.Dispose()` disposes the `Response`:** Check recent Azure.Core commits to see if `HttpMessage.Dispose()` was changed to call `Response.Dispose()`.\n   - Look at `Azure.Core/src/Pipeline/HttpMessage.cs` and check the `Dispose(bool)` override.\n\n2. **Check Azure.Core version shipped with Tables 12.11.0:** Determine if a recent Azure.Core bump introduced changed disposal semantics.\n\n3. **Confirm the fix is to buffer content before disposal:** In `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary`, one workaround is to load content via `BinaryData` instead of streaming from `ContentStream`. Alternatively, fix `GetEntityInternalAsync` to deserialize before returning from the scope.\n\n4. **Potential fix areas:**\n   - `TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync` — call `ResponseToDictionary` before the `Response` leaves the inner scope, or ensure the stream is buffered independently.\n   - `TableRestClient.QueryEntityWithPartitionAndRowKeyAsync` (custom) — extract the response content before the `using` block disposes the message.\n   - `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` — use `response.Content.ToMemory()` (reads from already-buffered `BinaryData`) instead of `response.ContentStream`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Reproduce in a local test environment (Azurite) with .NET 10 + Azure.Data.Tables 12.11.0\n- [ ] Verify if the bug reproduces on .NET 6/8 (to identify if this is a .NET 10 regression)\n- [ ] Audit `HttpMessage.Dispose()` in the version of `Azure.Core` shipped with Tables 12.11.0\n- [ ] Confirm root cause: does `HttpMessage.Dispose()` dispose the `ContentStream`?\n- [ ] Implement fix (likely in `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` or `GetEntityInternalAsync`)\n- [ ] Add a test covering `GetEntityAsync` / `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` on .NET 10\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Relevant Code Pointers\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n| File | Location | Notes |\n|------|----------|-------|\n| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableRestClient.cs` | Lines 177–188 | Custom protocol method — `using HttpMessage` disposes before caller deserializes |\n| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs` | Lines 733–810 | `GetEntityInternalAsync` — calls `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` after response returned |\n| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs` | Line 12–14 | `ResponseToDictionary` — reads `response.ContentStream` |\n| `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Generated/TableRestClient.cs` | Lines 610–650 | **Generated** path (not affected) — deserializes inside `using HttpMessage` scope |\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [Azure.Data.Tables source – TableClient.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs)\n- [Azure.Data.Tables source – SerializationHelpers.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs)\n- [Azure.Core HttpMessage (disposal)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/Pipeline/HttpMessage.cs)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n","item_number":57079},"name":"add_comment"}}}
